req.title: Overdraft protection (boundary variant)
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: overdraft linked

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-037 requires overdraft protection across three key partitions: (1) debit exceeds checking but savings covers it fully (positive/EP), (2) debit exceeds both checking and savings combined (negative/NEG), and (3) debit exactly equals the savings available balance (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'overdraft triggered' to 'fee assessed' (STC), and a fifth uses a decision table to cover the compound condition where checking is zero and savings partially covers (DT). Each test resets account state via fixtures to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_overdraft_draws_from_savings_when_checking_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=50.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=200.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee is configured at 35.00
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=100.00 against the checking account
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: savings account balance becomes 150.00 (50.00 drawn for shortfall)
overdraft_fee of 35.00 is assessed on the checking account
trace: R-FIN-037: linked savings debited when debit exceeds checking balance
R-FIN-037: fee assessed on successful overdraft draw
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_draws_from_savings_when_checking_insufficient(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):
    accounts = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=200.00
    )
    payload = {
        "account_id": accounts["checking_id"],
        "debit_amount": 100.00
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["result"] == "approved"
    assert body["overdraft_used"] is True

    checking = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}").json()
    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert checking["balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00)
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(150.00)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] == pytest.approx(35.00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_also_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=10.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=30.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=100.00 against the checking account
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-037: transaction declined when linked savings balance is insufficient
R-FIN-037: error pathway - declined when linked balance insufficient
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_also_insufficient(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):
    accounts = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=10.00,
        savings_balance=30.00
    )
    payload = {
        "account_id": accounts["checking_id"],
        "debit_amount": 100.00
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["result"] == "declined"
    assert "insufficient_funds" in body.get("reason", "")

    checking = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}").json()
    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert checking["balance"] == pytest.approx(10.00)
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(30.00)
    assert body.get("overdraft_fee", 0.00) == pytest.approx(0.00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_approved_when_debit_exactly_equals_savings_balance
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=0.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=75.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee is configured at 35.00
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=75.00 (exactly equal to savings available_balance)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: savings account balance becomes 0.00 (fully exhausted at boundary)
trace: R-FIN-037: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold of linked savings balance
R-FIN-037: linked savings debited up to its full available balance
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_approved_when_debit_exactly_equals_savings_balance(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):

        savings_balance=75.00
    )
    payload = {
        "account_id": accounts["checking_id"],
        "debit_amount": 75.00
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["result"] == "approved"
    assert body["overdraft_used"] is True

    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] == pytest.approx(35.00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_debit_exceeds_savings_by_one_cent
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=0.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=75.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=75.01 (one cent above savings available_balance)
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-037: boundary behaviour at max+1 threshold of linked savings balance
R-FIN-037: transaction declined when linked balance insufficient
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_when_debit_exceeds_savings_by_one_cent(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):
    accounts = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=0.00,
        savings_balance=75.00
    )
    payload = {
        "account_id": accounts["checking_id"],
        "debit_amount": 75.01
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["result"] == "declined"

    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(75.00)
    assert body.get("overdraft_fee", 0.00) == pytest.approx(0.00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transitions_from_triggered_to_fee_assessed
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=20.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=100.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Account transaction_state is 'normal' prior to debit
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=70.00 to trigger overdraft
GET /accounts/{checking_id} to verify state transitioned to 'overdraft_triggered'
expected: After overdraft POST, transaction_state transitions to 'overdraft_triggered'
savings debited by 50.00 (shortfall after 20.00 checking consumed)
trace: R-FIN-037: state transition normal -> overdraft_triggered -> fee_assessed
R-FIN-037: linked savings debited for shortfall amount
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_state_transitions_from_triggered_to_fee_assessed(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):
    accounts = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=20.00,
        savings_balance=100.00
    )
    pre = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}").json()
    assert pre["transaction_state"] == "normal"

    payload = {"account_id": accounts["checking_id"], "debit_amount": 70.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    post = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}").json()
    assert post["transaction_state"] == "overdraft_triggered"

    fees = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}/fees").json()
    assert fees["fee_state"] == "assessed"
    assert fees["amount"] == pytest.approx(35.00)

    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(50.00)

tests.name: test_overdraft_decision_table_no_checking_partial_savings_covers_exactly
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking_balance=0.00
Linked savings account exists with available_balance=55.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee is configured at 35.00
Decision table row: checking=0, savings>=debit, debit=55.00 -> approve
actions: Capture HTTP response and verify all decision outcomes
expected: entire debit covered by savings (savings drawn down to 0.00)
trace: R-FIN-037: decision table - checking=0, savings=debit amount, outcome=approve+fee
R-FIN-037: linked savings debited; fee assessed
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_decision_table_no_checking_partial_savings_covers_exactly(
    api_client,
    reset_accounts
):
    accounts = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=0.00,
        savings_balance=55.00
    )
    payload = {
        "account_id": accounts["checking_id"],
        "debit_amount": 55.00
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["result"] == "approved"
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] == pytest.approx(35.00)

    checking = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['checking_id']}").json()
    savings = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{accounts['savings_id']}").json()
    assert checking["balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00)
    assert savings["balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00)
